East Africa

Kenya Travel Guide

Best for first safari travelers, migration season planning, and itineraries that combine wildlife with a short coast extension.

General information about Kenya

Kenya remains the clearest first-time safari gateway thanks to strong guide networks, the Maasai Mara, private conservancies, and smooth access from Nairobi.

Getting around

Nairobi is the main hub for domestic bush flights, private transfers, and overland loops into the Mara, Amboseli, Laikipia, and the coast.

Best time to visit

July to October is peak migration season, while January to March is excellent for clearer weather and strong game viewing.

Planning notes

Use this country page to compare attractions, understand how to move between them, and decide whether the trip works best as a standalone route or part of a broader regional circuit.

Country at a glance

Kenya

  • 1 active tour operator profiles
  • 2 accommodations nearby
  • 2 recommended restaurants

Kenya

Maasai Mara

Kenya’s classic safari icon for migration drama, predators, and broad savannah horizons.

★ 4.9 (1,384) From $95

Amboseli National Park

Big-elephant country with unforgettable Kilimanjaro backdrops.

★ 4.8 (724) From $70
